sectiondef.header: mipi_tx_phy Flags to Enable or Disable core

briefdescription.para: Watchdog timeout in HS mode Register.
Goto Stop state on timeout timer Register.
Clk lane PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 0 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 1 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 2 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 3 PHY error Status Register.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_TX_PHY_CTRL_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used for the enabling/disabling and resetting the MIPI_TX_PHY

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_TX_PHY_INIT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used for lane Initialization.
Recommended to use 1ms or longer in for TX mode and 200us-500us for RX mode

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_TX_PHY_HSTIMEOUT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to program watchdog timer in high speed mode.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_TX_PHY_ESCTIMEOUT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains Rx Data Lanes timeout for watchdog timer in escape mode.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_TX_PHY_CLSTATUS_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the clock lane status and state machine control.

detaileddescription.para: Combined mask of all clock lane status bits including error control, stop state, initialization done, ULPS, and mode bits

briefdescription.para: Combined mask of all clock lane status bits including error control, stop state, initialization done, ULPS, and mode bits.
Bit offset for Control Error on Clock.
Bit offset for Stop State on Clock.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_TX_PHY_DLxSTATUS_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the data lanes status

briefdescription.para: Set on Data Lane Esc timeout occurs.
Set on Data Lane HS timeout.
Set when MIPI_TX_PHY in ULPS mode.
Control Mode (Esc, Low, High) of Data Lane.
Combined mask for all data lane status bits.

detaileddescription.para: Combined mask of all data lane status bits including mode, ULPS, initialization done, HS abort, escape abort, stop state, calibration status, and calibration complete bits

briefdescription.para: Bit offset for High Speed Abort.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_TX_PHY_HSSETTLE_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to program the HS SETTLE register.

briefdescription.para: Programmable sequence symbol positions for C-PHY mode.

detaileddescription.para: Enumeration of symbol indices used to program the C-PHY programmable sequence registers. Symbols 0-9 map to DATA0 register, symbols 10-13 map to DATA1 register.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific Mipi_Tx_Phy instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: Configure the registers of the Mipi_Tx_Phy instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
to one of the registers to be configured
to be set for the particular Handle of the Mipi_Tx_Phy instance

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE If incorrect handle was passed

simplesect.para: There is a limit on the minimum and maximum values of the HS Timeout register.

briefdescription.para: Program a symbol value into the programmable sequence table.

detaileddescription.para: This helper masks in the 3-bit symbol value into the correct programmable sequence register (DATA0 or DATA1) based on the requested symbol index. The programmable sequence control is temporarily disabled to avoid partial writes.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.
is the programmable sequence symbol index.
is the 3-bit symbol value to program.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the symbol was programmed.
XST_FAILURE if the control register remained enabled and the write was aborted.

simplesect.para: The core must be configured for C-PHY mode (IsDphy == 0) and register access must be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.
is the programmable sequence symbol index.

simplesect.para: Register contents of DATA0 (symbols 0-9) or DATA1 (symbols 10-13).
The core must be configured for C-PHY mode (IsDphy == 0) and register access must be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.

simplesect.para: The core must be configured for C-PHY mode (IsDphy == 0) and register access must be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.

simplesect.para: The core must be configured for C-PHY mode (IsDphy == 0) and register access must be enabled.

briefdescription.para: Read the programmable sequence control register.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.

simplesect.para: Current contents of the programmable sequence control register.
The core must be configured for C-PHY mode (IsDphy == 0) and register access must be enabled.

briefdescription.para: Get if register interface is present from the config structure for specified Mipi_Tx_Phy instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Get information stored in the Mipi_Tx_Phy instance based on the handle passed.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
to one of the registers to be configured

simplesect.para: The value stored in the corresponding register

briefdescription.para: This is used to do a soft reset of the Mipi_Tx_Phy IP instance.

detaileddescription.para: The reset takes approx 20 core clock cycles to become effective.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This is used to clear the Abort Error (Escape or High Speed) bits in the Data Lane 0 through 3.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
represents which Data Lane to act upon
contains information about which bits to reset

briefdescription.para: This is used to get information about Clock Lane status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing which of the events have occurred along with the mode of the Clock Lane in DPhy

briefdescription.para: This is used to get specific Lane mode information about Clock Lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Clock Lane in Mipi_Tx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get information about a Data Lane status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the status is sought for.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing which of the events have occurred along with the mode of the Data Lane in DPhy

briefdescription.para: This is used to get Data Lane Calibration status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the calib status is sought for.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS - Calibration Complete, Calibration packet received XST_NO_DATA - Calibration Complete, Calibration packet is not received XST_FAILURE - Calibration failed

briefdescription.para: This is used to get specific Lane mode information about a Data Lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the mode info is requested.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Data Lane in Mipi_Tx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get count of packets received on each lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the mode info is requested.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Data Lane in Mipi_Tx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get Mipi_Tx_Phy Version.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Returns major and minor Version number of this Mipi_Tx_Phy IP

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable or disable the Mipi_Tx_Phy core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance to operate on.
denoting whether to enable or disable the Mipi_Tx_Phy core

briefdescription.para: This inline function is used to set bit in a mipi_tx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field to be set

briefdescription.para: This inline function is used to reset bit in a mipi_tx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field to be reset

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XMipi_Tx_Phy_GetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of bitfield from mipi_tx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field whose value needs to be obtained
is offset of bit field

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XMipi_Tx_Phy_SetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the value of bitfield from mipi_tx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field whose value needs to be updated
is offset of bit field
to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: Look up the hardware configuration for a device instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the BaseAddress of the device to lookup for

simplesect.para: The reference to the configuration record in the configuration table (in xmipi_tx_phy_g.c) corresponding to the BaseAddr or if not found,a NULL pointer is returned.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test checks if HS Timeout value present in register matches the one from the generated file.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMipi_Tx_Phy instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the read value was not equal to _g.c file

briefdescription.para: This function reads a value from a MIPI_TX_PHY register space.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a MIPI_TX_PHY register space A 32 bit write is performed.

detaileddescription.para: If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: Timeout value in loop iterations for soft reset completion.

detaileddescription.para: Ensures at least 20 core clock cycles forreset to take effect.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Register offset between consecutive data lane status registers.
This file implements the functions to control and get info from the mipi_tx_phy.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
Contains diagnostic/self-test functions for the XMipi_Tx_Phy component.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xmipi_tx_phy_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by the user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release
